symbols: white-winged figure — an angelic being, messenger of God
white garments — purity and heavenly origin
description: A painting of a young woman or girl with large white wings, wearing a white dress, standing frontally against a muted background. The figure has a serene, otherworldly quality. The wings are rendered with naturalistic detail while the overall effect is ethereal and devotional.
theological_description: Angels are 'ministering spirits sent out to serve for the sake of those who are to inherit salvation' (HEB.1.14). The white-winged figure reflects the biblical description of angelic beings as messengers of God, appearing throughout Scripture from Genesis to Revelation. Angels announced Christ's birth (LUK.2.9-14), ministered to him in the wilderness (MAT.4.11), and proclaimed his resurrection (MAT.28.5-6).

scripture_refs.reason: Angels as ministering spirits serving the heirs of salvation
He will command his angels concerning you
